brief_facts:
IN THE COURT OF HONOURABLE SPECIAL JUDICIAL FIRST CLASS MAGISTRATE FOR EXCISE CASES AT GANDHI BHAWAN, HYDERABAD.
Honoured Sir,
Facts of the case are that Today i.e., on 31.05.2024 at 0515 hours received an English typed complaint from Sri G Srinivas PC 3139 of PS Bahadurpura, Hyderabad. Would like to submit that on 30/05/2024 at 20:00 hrs he reported for night duty. On SHO Sri Instructions section in charge officer deputed him on BC-1 duty. While he was patrolling in the BC-1 area, on 31/05/2024 at 00:30 hrs he was patrolling at Hassan nagar main road area that mean time three suspected persons are in passenger auto while he reached the auto to know were and about, the auto driver by looking at him, he drive his auto in a rash manner and run away from the spot towards Hassan nagar, badanalla. Then he followed auto along with patroll Car-1, by looking at their auto driver stopped his auto at united school, Hassan nagar, and all are run away from there, then they reached auto, and searched in auto found 18 ganja plastic packets and its whole weight about 100 grams approximately, after that with the help of local persons brought the auto B No. TS 15 UD 9137 to PS Bahadurpura to handover for further investigation. Hence he request to kind office to take legal action as per law. 
Received on 31.05.2024 at 0515 Hours:
Basing on the contents of complaint a case has been registered in Cr No. 162/2024 U/s 8(c)r/w 20(b)(ii)(B) of NDPS Act 1985 and entrusted investigation to Sri A Srikanth, SI.
Sd/-
M A Kareem,
Asst Sub-Inspector of Police,
PS Bahadurpura, Hyd.

H.no. 13-6-455/7, Heera Nagar, Gudimalkapur, Hyd , 8) B Ratan Singh @ Ratan S/o Bhatela Kishan Bahadar, Age: 22 years, Occ: Labor, R/o: H.no. 12-2-823/A/51, Santhosh nagar Colony, Mehdipatnam, Cell:6300803043, 9) Eriki Pavan, S/o Eriki Raju, Age : 28 Years, Occ : CC Road Labour work, R/o. H.no. 12-2-708/1/9/VVN/109, Viveka Nanda Nagar, Bhojagutta, Hyderabad as these persons found suspicious in their behavior. Then I caused enquiries with the above said persons and initially they gave evasive replies but on further continuous questioning with them, they voluntarily disclosed that they are habituated to consumption of Ganja. Then I informed to them that consuming Ganja and other drugs, which is an offence under NDPS Act. Later, I have sent the above said persons to OGH, Hyderabad under police escort for conducting drug test on them, accordingly OGH doctors conducted drugs test on them. Out of (09) persons, four persons i.e., 1) Mohd Akheelullah @ Akheel S/o Mohd Shafeeullah, age: 36 years, Occ: Helmets Business, r/o H.No. 13-6-431/C/25, Moghalnagar, Near Pillar No. 100, Hyderabad,2) Batala Mansingh @ Teja S/o Kishan Singh age: 28 yrs Occ: Private Employee R/o 12-2-823/A/51, Santosh Nagar Colony, Mehdipatnam, and Hyderabad, 3) B Ratan Singh @ Ratan S/o Bhatela Kishan Bahadar, Age: 22 years, Occ: Labor, R/o: H.No. 12-2-823/A/51, Santhosh nagar Colony, Mehdipatnam, , 4) Zaid Bin Naser @ Zahed S/o Naser Bin Ahmed, age: 39 years, Occ: Driver, R/o. H. No: 13-6-431/C/57, Moghal Nagar, Ring Road, and Pillar No: 102, Karwan, Attapur, and Hyderabad and the remaining five persons got negative. 

On further questioning with them, they voluntarily admitted that they are habituated to consumption of Ganja and Opiate and Benzodiazepine willing to undergo treatment and rehabilitation if they are given opportunity. The accused persons further stated that they are willing to give requisitions before the Hon'ble Court to consider their request that they may kindly be referred to the Government of Telangana authorized De-addiction center for a duration as the Hon'ble Court deems fit as per the provisions of Sec 64A of the NDPS Act, which allows immunity from prosecution for addicts volunteering for treatment. Hence, I am herewith producing the above said persons with a request to taken necessary action on them U/s 27(b) NDPS Act.
Thanking you,
                                                                                                              Yours faithfully
                                                                                                                     Sd/-
                                                                                                           ( G. Bajiya Naik)

As per the contents of the above report, I, registered a case in Cr. No: 134/2026 U/Sec: 27(b) of NDPS ACT and took up the investigation.

                                                Inspector of Police
                                             PS Gudimalkapur, Hyd.

          Then, he caused enquiries with the suspects about their presence here at place, beside Nala, near MCH Quarters Kavadiguda, initially they gave evasive replies, but on continuous questioning, they revealed that they have been addicted to consuming/smoking Ganja, by procuring from one unknown person at Dhoolpet, Hyderabad. He informed the same to his immediate superior officer, Inspector of Police, P.S Gandhi Nagar.
          He informed the suspects that possession, transportation, sale, purchase, and consumption of Ganja constitute offences punishable under the NDPS Act 1985. Then he questioned them whether they having any contraband on their personal possession and they stated that they are not carrying any contraband in their personal possession. As such the services of a Gazetted Officer or Magistrate under Section 50 of NDPS Act were not taken in this case. 
          Then he handed over one drug testing kit to each suspect marked with their name individually and obtained signature on the same.  They were instructed to proceed to the washroom (open palace beside nala) and provide  urine samples in the said kit. Accordingly, the accused persons returned with the drug testing kit containing urine sample. The clues team incharge observed the results of the drug testing kits which indicated a ‘Positive’ result for the consumption of Ganja to the above person.

Syed fazal, age 29 years and 7) Harshavardhan, s/o. Srinivasa Rao, age 27 years with a request to take necessary action against them under Section 27(b) of the NDPS Act.

address: India
brief_facts:
IN THE COURT OF HON’BLE X ADDL. CHIEF JUDICIAL MAGISTRATE AT SEC’BAD.

Honored Sir,
The brief facts of the case are on that on 06.04.2026 at 21.00 hrs recieved a petition from Sri. C Chandraiah, SI of police, PS Chilkalguda, Hyderabad, Cell No: 9908612324 today ie., on 06.04.2026 at about 18:00 hours, while he was present at the Police Station, he received credible and reliable information that one person was in possession of a psychotropic substance, namely MDMA (Methamphetamine), and was attempting to sell the same to customers at an open place near South Central Railway Employees Sangh Office, Chilkalguda, Secunderabad. Believing the said information to be true and actionable, he reduced the same into writing and forthwith informed his superior officer under Section 42(2) of the NDPS Act. A General Diary entry was made to that effect, and the information was duly communicated to the superior officer in compliance with the mandatory provisions of law. Thereafter, he secured the presence of two independent mediators:
(1) Sri P. Anil, S/o P. Dharamdas, Age: 46 years, Occ: Business, R/o H. No.          7-1-312, Maruti Nagar, Monda Market, Secunderabad, Cell No. 8977642649;
(2) Sri David, S/o Domnik, Age: 39 years, Occ: Business, R/o near Ayyappa Swamy Temple, Mettuguda, Secunderabad, Cell No. 9553101044. He also informed Sri. V. Ramesh, CSO, Clues Team, Mahankali Division, Hyderabad, over phone and requested him to come along with a videographer for identification and documentation of the contraband. At about 18:40 hours, upon arrival of mediators and Clues Team, he along with his staff namely (1) PC D. Krishna Kumar (9746), (2) PC Ganesh (31243), (3) PC Mohd. Azher Uddin (3643) [Writer], (4) PC Sk. Madar Basha (1893), and mediators, left the Police Station after making a GD entry, carrying necessary equipment such as weighing machine, packing material, laptop, and mini printer, in Government Vehicle No. TS-09-PA-5130 driven by HG Pramod Kumar (5958). They reached South Central Railway Employees Sangh Office, Chilkalguda, Secunderabad area at about 19:00 hours, and upon confirmation by the informant, proceeded to the spot i.e., open place near South Central Railway Employees Sangh Office, Chilkalguda, Secunderabad. He instructed Sri Jhony Reddy, HG 4507, Clues Team, to conduct continuous videography of the entire proceedings from entry till completion. At the spot, they apprehended one male person, and upon questioning he disclosed his identity as Busa Sai Nikil @ Sai Yadav, S/o Busa Shyam Prasad Yadav, Age: 18 years 2 months (D.O.B: 31-01-2008), Occ: Private Job, R/o H. No. 7-130, Chintal, Ambedkar Nagar, Road No. 27, Balanagar, Hyderabad, Cell No. 6303776961. He was informed about the information received and the purpose of the visit. Before conducting search, he was clearly informed of his legal right under Section 50 of the NDPS Act to be searched in the presence of a Gazetted Officer or a Magistrate. However, after understanding the said right, he voluntarily declined and stated that no contraband was concealed on his person and that he did not require the presence of such officer. The same was recorded in the presence of mediators. During sustained interrogation, the accused voluntarily confessed that he had procured MDMA (Methamphetamine) at the instance of one Aditya, R/o Chintal, Balanagar, Hyderabad, and was supplying the same to customers in Hyderabad for monetary gain, apart from consuming it himself. He further disclosed that he has been indulging in such illegal activity for the past three (03) months to earn easy money and has been addicted to the consumption of MDMA for the past six (06) months, having been influenced by the said Aditya and other local associates. 
Pursuant to his voluntary disclosure, the accused produced a yellow colour polythene carry bag containing a metal box, inside which two zip-lock packets were found—one containing powder substance and the other containing crystalline substance. The Clues Team tested the samples using field testing kit, which confirmed the substance as MDMA (Methamphetamine). The contraband was marked as P-1 and P-2. P-1 weighed 41.91 grams and P-2 weighed 75.39 grams, totalling 117.39 grams, which falls under commercial quantity. The entire seized property was weighed, packed, sealed with lac, labeled, and signatures of mediators, accused, and complainant were obtained on the sealed packets in accordance with law.
 	The accused further voluntarily disclosed the details of the customers/consumers to whom he has been supplying the contraband, as follows:
1.	Gaddam Naveen Kumar, S/o Gaddam Krishna – Cell No. 8125329053 
2.	Kerala Mahesh Kumar, S/o Satyanarayana – Cell No. 6303858523 
3.	Penta Rohit Kumar, S/o Satyanarayana – Cell No. 9133030645 
4.	Devarakonda Surya, S/o Anjaneyulu – Cell No. 9177263745 
 	 	The above persons are suspected to be consumers/associates, and efforts are being made to trace and apprehend them along with the main supplier, who are presently absconding. The accused was informed of the grounds of arrest and was taken into custody in accordance with law. Arrest intimation was duly served. The entire proceedings were videographed by the Clues Team to ensure transparency and evidentiary value. The act of the accused clearly attracts offences punishable under Sections 8(c) r/w 22(c), 27 NDPS Act, as he was found in conscious possession of commercial quantity of psychotropic substance and involved in illegal trafficking. Hence, he herewith produced the accused Busa Sai Nikil @ Sai Yadav along with seized contraband and request that necessary legal action may kindly be taken against Busa Sai Nikil and his associates as per law.
-: Original English written petition is enclosed herewith:-
Received on 06.04.2026 at 21.00 hrs:
        As per the contents of the above complaint I, L. Madhu Babu, Detective Inspector of Police registered a case in Cr No: 165/2026 U/s 8(c), r/w 22(c), 27 of NDPS Act, and investigation entrusted to Sri. V. Ramakrishna, Inspector of Police.
               Sd/-
(Sri L. Madhu Babu, Detective Inspector of Police, PS Chilkalguda)

Today i.e, on 24.12.2025 at about 0700Hours the complainant received a credible information that four persons are transporting the dry ganja in two vehicles and  are reportedly traveling to Chennai  from Rajahmundry via Jangareddygudem, Ashwaraopeta, Dammapeta, and V.M. Banjara towards Vijayawada. Then after informed to the Immediate officer and making GD Entry , the Compolainant along with the staff conducted vehicle checking and caught hold the two vehicles and apprehended the accused A1: Vanthala Emalayya r/o Chintapalle, Vishakapatnam, Andhra Pradesh (Transporter), A2: Sanmugam M r/o Kayalarmedu  Chinnaobulapuram Village of Tamilnadu (Transporter) A3: Arjun B r/o Peria Obulapuram Village  of Tamilnadu (Transporter), , A4: Devakar r/o Pondavakkam Village  of Tamilnadu (Transporter) while they Illegally Transporting The Dry Ganja which was bought from the accused A6) Sadu Guruji, 60 yrs R/o.Chitrakonda , of Orissa Stat (Supplier) and which was  intended to hand over to the accused A5). Vikram r/o .Red Hills,Chennai, Tamilnadu (Receiver) and also seized  152 Packets of dry ganga weighing approximately 304 Kgs , (Per Kg Rs.50,000/-) Total W/Rs 1,52,00,000/-  from the two seized vehicles 1) Maruti Suzuki Ertiga car bearing RC No. AP 14 EC 5834 which belongs to A8: Vikas Ankush Tangade r/o Lonwadi, TQ Sillod, Aurangabad, Maharastra(Supplier and owner of the escorting maruthi Suzuki Ertiga Car Bearing No:AP 14 EC 5834)  and 2) 6-tyre Tata Motors Ltd goods carrier vehicle( Closed Container)  bearing RC No. TN-73-AD-9515 which belongs to A7: J.Subramanayam r/o Karumariamman of Tamilnadu ( Receiver and owner of Crime vehicle i.e Goods carrier container Bearing No: TN 73 AD 9515)) and also seized 5 mobile phones, Rs.15,000/- Cash(1.Rs.9,000/- from A1 & Rs.6,000/- from A3) ,Train ticket and Parking Receipt from the posession of the Accused under a cover of Panchanama in presence of two mediators, clues team and Tamil translater and brought to PS along with the dry ganga, accused A1 to A4  and seized material. The accused all together colluded with each other and selling the Ganja to innocent general public and gaining illegal money and to get more profits.

brief_facts:
IN THE COURT OF HONOURABLE XII ADDITIONAL CHIEF
                                        JUDICIAL MAGISTRATE, AT NAMPALLY
Honoured Madam,                                                                            
   	Brief facts of the case on 10-04-2026 at 0220 hrs  received a complaint from Sri. P Sai Kumar SI of Police, Nampally PS, Hyderabad. In which he stated that he, the undersigned submit that, on 09.04.2026 at about 2115 hours, While he performing the patrolling duty along with staff PC 30859 B. Vijaykanth Reddy, PC 5258 M. Gopinath, PC 5279 N. Krishna and PC 6354 C. Kranthi kumar. Upon reaching the abandoned railway bridge, Near Horticulture, Nampally, Hyderabad and they found four persons in suspicious circumstances. No drugs were found in their possession at that time. On enquiry, the individuals revealed their identities as follows: 1. Padira Rohith S/o: Raghu, age: 26yrs, Occ: Tatoo Artist, Caste: Munnurukapu (BC-D), R/o: H.No. 3-5-948/118, Gandhi Kutir, Street No.6, Narayanaguda, Hyderabad, N/o: Quarters No. SD-158, bhagath Singh Nagar, Ramakrishnapuram, Manchiryal (Vill & Town), Goadavari Kani District, Ph No.8143807080, 9949839834, 2. Minikikar Sailu, S/o: Balakrishna, age: 25yrs, Occ: Wallet Parking at Crona Enbuilt, Kothaguda, Caste: SC (Mochi), R/o: H.No.5-8-448, Chirag Ali Lane, Abids, Hyderabad, Ph No. 9346783723, 7794806639, 3. Premji Thangam Benny, S/o: Benny, age: 25yrs, Occ: Telecom Airtel Employee, Caste: Christian, R/o: Airtel Office, Rasoolpura, Hyderabad, N/o: Mudimarthil (V), Kargokabhali (PS), Kottayam Dist, Keral State, PIN-626540, Ph No.7559098897, 7997551920, 4. Jebin Pulikunnai po Josh, S/o: Josh Pulikunnai Yohanan, age: 26yrs, Occ: Telecom Airtel Employee, Caste: Christian, R/o: Airtel Office, Rasoolpura, Hyderabad, N/o: Kothala (V & Post), Pambadi (PS), Kottayam Dist, Keralam State, PIN-626540, Ph No.9539739166, 9048626880. Upon questioning, all four admitted that they had consumed drugs a few days earlier and also informed that they addicted to drugs and they were unable to come out of it. Then he immediately sent the four individuals to Gandhi Hospital, Secunderabad, where they were subjected to medical examination using 12-panel test kits. The results confirmed drug consumption. Padira Rohith tested positive for THC-Marijuana and weakly positive for BZO-Benzodiazepines, TCA Tricyclic Antidepressants. Minikikar Sailu tested positive for THC-Marijuana. Premji Thangam Benny tested weakly positive for BZO-Benzodiazepines, TCA Tricyclic Antidepressants. Jebin Pulikunnai po Josh tested weakly positive for BZO-Benzodiazepines, TCA Tricyclic Antidepressants. Consequently, on 10.04.2026 at about 0050 hrs, they were brought to Nampally Police Station and it is therefore requested that legal action be initiated against them.
                          Thanking You 

                                                                            Yours faithfully,
                                                                                    Sd/-
                                                                            (P Sai Kumar)
                                                                       Sub-Inspector of Police,
                                                                   PS Nampally, Hyderabad.
                                                                          Ph.No: 8712660172			      
  Received on 10.04.2026 at 02:20 hrs
 As per the contents of the above complaint, I registered a case in Cr.No 126/2026, U/s   8(c) r/w  27(b) NDPS Act and took up the investigation                                                                               
                                                    
                                                                                 Sd/-
                          (P Sai Kumar Sub-Inspector of Police, P.S. Nampally, Hyderabad).
                                    //The Original written complaint enclosed herewith//

In which he stated that “ On 06.08.2025, at around 10:00 AM, while he was in charge of the police station, he received a reliable information that two males, one wearing a red T-shirt, light green lower point and carrying a light pink college bag, and the other wearing a blue checked full shirt and black jeans, both on a bike, with a bag containing packets of dried ganja were going to sell it at Kushigutta on the outskirts of Palakurthy between 11:00 AM and 12:00 PM. On that he informed the same to his superior officer through the telephone.   Further he sent a requisition to the Tahsildar, Executive Magistrate at Palakurthy with a request to send two persons to act as panch witnesses.  He also secured the presence of photographer namely Rapolu Rambabu, S/ o.Lingaiah, Age: 42 yrs, Caste: Padmasali, Occ: Photographer, R/o. Palakurthy, Cell:9866061591 and also another  person namely Bonagiri Vidyasagar, S/o.Rangayya Age:49 years, Caste:Vaishya Occ: Grocery shop, R/o Palakurthy. Cell: 944006213 to weigh the ganja. He along with the police personnel 1) V. Raj Kumar, PC-1362, 2) B. Ramesh, PC-1265, 3) M. Surya Prakash PC-10615, 4).P. Yakaiah PC-1373, Station Writer, left Palakurthy police station at 10:40 am in police vehicle bearing number TS09PA5392 with driver G. Amar Singh ARPC- 2948 and reached Kushigutta on the outskirts of Palakurthy at 10:50 am. While they were all looking for people who were selling and buying ganja, there were seven men under a tree with a college bag and a bike. When they saw them, they tried to run away. He along with his staff apprehended them and asked them why they were running away. On that they said that they were running away because they were afraid of being caught because they had dry ganja.  On that he enquired about the details and identity of the said persons in the presence of panch witnesses. On that they revealed their names and other details as 1). Vadlakonda Sabarinath, S/o.Ramesh, Age: 25 years, Caste: Gowda, Occupation: Driver, R/o Kothapalli village, Ghanpur[Stn] mandal, Jangaon district, Phone number: 7995077506, 2). Govindu Srinath, S/o.Chandraiah, Age: 23, years, Caste: Madiga, Occ: welding work, R/o Kothapalli village, Ghanpur[Stn] mandal, Jangaon district, phone number: 7995077506, 3). Guggilla Praveen, S/o.Srisailam, Age: 24 years, Caste: Madiga, Occ: Driver, R/o Gudur village, Palakurthi mandal, Jangaon district, phone number: 9512014479,4).Gunde Naveen, S/o. Yadagiri, Age: 25 years, Caste: Madiga, Occ: laborer, R/o Samudrala village, Ghanpur[stn] mandal, Jangaon district, phone number: 9642382882, 5) Gundamalla Sampath, S/o.Kumaraswamy, Age: 18 years, Caste: Mala, Occ: student, R/o Gudur village, Palakurthi mandal, Jangaon district, phone number: 9346308872, 6). Banda Akhil, S/o.Narasimha, Age: 19 years, Caste: Bikshakuntla, occupation: Coolie, R/o Palakurthy village & mandal Janagaon district, phone number: 6302591651, 7). Cheeturi Shyam, S/o.Chandrayya, Age: 25 years, Caste: Madiga, Occ: Painting work, R/o. Palakurthi village & mandal Jangaon district, phone number: 6302591651 and he recorded the confessional statement of the above said persons into writing, and seized the dry ganja weighing about 1.340 Kgs, W/Rs.65,000/-, packed in 7 brown packets by marking as M-1 to M-7, the bag used to carry the brown packets was marked as P-8 and marked the empty covers as P-1 to P-7 by placing them in a white colored polythene bag, marking it as M-8 in the presence of panch witnesses,6 mobile phones & 1 Hero deluxe bike. The processes of seizure of dry ganja was got photographed by Rapolu Rambabu.the absconding accused 8) Ujjwal Sai Kumar Raju @ Nani who supplied dried ganja to Vadlakonda Sabarinath & Govindu Srinath. Afterwards he returned to PS Palakurthy and lodged a report before the SHO with a request to take necessary action against the said person as per law.”
